meta.title: Unit 1 — Singular & Plural Nouns (Test 3)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Write and identify plural nouns using -s and -es rules.

questions.question: What is the plural of "cookie"?
questions.explanation: cookie → cookies. When a noun ends in a vowel + e, just add -s.

questions.question: "Pencil" is a singular noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! "pencil" means one pencil — singular.

questions.question: Write the plural of "dog".
questions.explanation: dog → dogs. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Joe watches a snail crawl down a ___. (log)
questions.explanation: "a" tells us we need a singular noun: log (one log).

questions.question: The plural of "fox" is "foxes".
questions.explanation: Yes! fox ends in -x, so we add -es: fox → foxes.

questions.question: Write the plural of "bottle".
questions.explanation: bottle → bottles. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Roy drinks a ___ of juice. (glass)
questions.explanation: "a" tells us we need a singular noun: a glass of juice.

questions.question: Sort these words: Singular or Plural?
questions.explanation: house/pencil = one (singular). rulers/cats/buses = more than one (plural).

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "She has three boxes."

questions.question: In the sentence "Mom buys seven apples," the noun is plural.
questions.explanation: "apples" is plural — seven tells us there is more than one.

questions.question: Write the plural of "class".
questions.explanation: class → classes. Words ending in -ss add -es.

questions.question: Which word uses the correct plural rule?
questions.explanation: watch ends in -ch, so we add -es: watch → watches.

questions.question: Sort these plurals: did they add -s or -es?
questions.explanation: Regular nouns add -s. Nouns ending in -sh or -x add -es.

questions.question: "Ants" is the correct plural form of "ant".
questions.explanation: Yes! ant → ants. A regular noun — just add -s.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: "Four girls dance to the music."

questions.question: Write the plural of "toe".
questions.explanation: toe → toes. Regular nouns add -s.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: "Some" tells us we need a plural: bears. bear → bears.

questions.question: What is the plural of "cockroach"?
questions.explanation: cockroach ends in -ch, so we add -es: cockroach → cockroaches.

questions.question: In the sentence "Three rocks are by the tree," the noun "rocks" is singular.
questions.explanation: "Three rocks" means more than one rock — that's plural, not singular.

questions.question: Write the plural of "glass".
questions.explanation: glass → glasses. Words ending in -ss add -es.
